phase3_findings.description: The resume-arm absence pin — guarding THE load-bearing invariant (never flip on the green resume path, which would disarm the resumed run's backstop) — keyed its awk range on literal 12-space `resume)` / 14-space `;;`. On any reindent the range never opens, awk prints nothing, and the absence check reports PASS with the regression present. Proven by mutation.
devflow.yml's CAUSE-derivation branch is neither pinned nor tested.

The `! grep -q "'failed':"` pin guarding the AC "Failed remains absent from _STATUS_TO_PROGRESS_PHASE" is evaded by a double-quoted `"failed":` key, and the whole suite stays GREEN with that regression present (the behavioral note-nesting test's fixture has no Review row, so a failed->'Review' mapping falls through to the same last-ticked fallback). The grep pin was the only guard, and it was evadable.
Filed the test_python_scripts.py comment's rationale as a documented_falsehood, asserting the behavioral note-nesting test WOULD catch a failed->'Review' mapping.
_resume_arm_body's flat awk range would close at the first `;;` of a NESTED case inside the resume arm, re-opening the fail-open. Not reachable today (the arm is flat).

fix_decisions.claim_text: the behavioral note-nesting test does not catch that either (its fixture has no Review row)
fix_decisions.evidence: The documented_falsehood grading fails its own discriminator (false-against-HEAD). Observable evidence: (a) BEFORE the semantic assertion existed, inserting `"failed": 'Review'` into _STATUS_TO_PROGRESS_PHASE left the ENTIRE suite green — so no existing behavioral test catches it; (b) comment-analyzer independently read the #356 note-nesting fixture at lib/test/run.sh:4149-4165 and confirmed its Progress rows are only Setup + Implement, no Review row; (c) code-reviewer independently verified the claim TRUE; (d) pr-test-analyzer's mutation matrix shows ONLY the new semantic assertion goes RED. The final-pass reviewer evaluated different fixtures in test_python_scripts.py and hypothetical tests ('the two fixtures a Failed note test WOULD naturally use'), not the test the comment names. Residual: the comment is ambiguous about which file's fixture -> a clarity Suggestion, surfaced for the human. Not edited, per the post-shadow-edit gate's rule that the shadow-reviewed commit is the deliverable.

fix_decisions.claim_text: _resume_arm_body's flat awk range would truncate at a nested case's first `;;`
fix_decisions.evidence: Corroborated by two delta-review reviewers, but unreachable against the shipped workflow: the resume) arm (devflow-implement.yml:852-869) contains no nested case and no `;;` in its body, verified by grep. A structural change that introduced one would need to re-verify the pin. Surfaced for the human rather than pre-emptively hardened against a construction that does not exist.

shadow.trigger: convergence-time (2377cd8), plus two bounded post-shadow delta-reviews over 2377cd8..61158b5 and 61158b5..cb2090a; reviewed_sha advanced to HEAD after each returned no confirmed new Critical/Important
